name: OpenMCAS Hypothesis Agent
description: An open, MIT-licensed hypothesis-generation agent for MCAS / MCAD (Mast Cell Activation Syndrome / Disorder). Curates a compound library across pharma, herbs, supplements, and AI-generated analogs; runs ligand-based virtual screening, covalent-warhead detection, ADMET QSAR, and multi-objective ranking across rescue / maintenance / remission categories. Every prediction is published openly so any researcher can audit, falsify, or extend it.

provider.legalEntity: MR Dula Medical, a DBA of MR Dula Enterprise, LLC

disclaimers.medicalAdvice: This agent generates research hypotheses only. It does not diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any condition. It is not a substitute for clinical care. See https://github.com/mrdulasolutions/MCAS.Opensource/blob/main/docs/disclaimers.md.
disclaimers.selfExperimentation: Outputs of this agent must not be acted on for self-treatment. Patients should work with a mast-cell specialist.

status.manifestPurpose: Describes the conceptual A2A agent interface. The underlying skills are implemented as scripts in the repository and runnable on a CPU laptop in ~3 minutes. A read-only browse interface to current predictions is live as a public Hugging Face Space (browseOnlyViewer above). A hosted A2A endpoint is on the project roadmap (see ROADMAP.md).

extensions.x-openmcas/dataProvenance: PubChem PUG-REST, AlphaFold DB, PyTDC, hand-curated

skills.name: Search the MCAS compound library
skills.description: Search the curated MCAS/MCAD compound library by name, PubChem CID, category (rescue / maintenance / remission), target, mechanism, or evidence level. Returns matched entries with SMILES, mechanism, and evidence.
examples: List all rescue compounds with high evidence
Find compounds targeting KEAP1
Get the SMILES for sulforaphane
Which compounds in the library are mast cell stabilizers?

skills.name: Propose a new compound for the library
skills.description: Propose a pharma drug, herb, supplement, or biologic for the curated library. Validates the PubChem CID, fetches the canonical SMILES, and opens a GitHub PR to data/compounds/seeds.json with the proposed entry.
examples: Add azelastine to the library as a maintenance H1 antagonist
Propose adding moringin (CID 73895174) under remission with KEAP1 target

skills.name: Report an MCAS trigger pattern
skills.description: Submit an anonymous trigger pattern (food / smell / physical / stress / infection / toxin / hormonal / medication) for inclusion in the trigger -> pathway -> counter-compound map. No PHI is collected; only the pattern + onset + duration + dominant symptom domain.
examples: Report that perfumes trigger immediate skin flushing
Log a trigger pattern for aged cheese and GI symptoms

skills.name: Ligand-based virtual screening against MCAS targets
skills.description: Given one or more SMILES, compute max Tanimoto similarity (Morgan radius 2, 2048 bits) against curated reference ligand sets for 8 MCAS targets: MRGPRX2, KIT, KEAP1, HRH1, HRH2, CYSLTR1, BTK, GLP1R.
examples: Score this SMILES against MRGPRX2 and KIT
Run ligand-based screen for CS(=O)CCCCN=C=S across all targets

skills.name: Covalent warhead + KEAP1 pharmacophore scoring
skills.description: Apply 13 cysteine-reactive SMARTS patterns (isothiocyanate, Michael acceptor, cyanoenone, acrylamide, vinyl sulfone, epoxide, quinone, etc.) plus a KEAP1 pharmacophore filter (MW, cLogP, heavy atoms). Returns warhead presence + KEAP1 pharmacophore pass + a [0,1] warhead score.
examples: Does this molecule have a cysteine-reactive warhead?
Score this SMILES for KEAP1 pharmacophore

skills.name: ADMET QSAR prediction (hERG, AMES, BBB)
skills.description: Predict cardiac liability (hERG), mutagenicity (AMES), and blood-brain barrier penetration (BBB_Martins) for a SMILES using RandomForest classifiers trained on PyTDC tasks (validation AUC 0.89-0.91).
examples: Predict hERG / AMES / BBB for this SMILES
Is this molecule predicted to cross the blood-brain barrier?

skills.name: Generate SFN-class candidate analogs
skills.description: Enumerate candidate analogs of the sulforaphane class using RDKit BRICS recombination + bioisosteric substitution + warhead grafting onto library scaffolds. Seeds: SFN, iberin, erucin, sulforaphene, allyl-ITC, benzyl-ITC, phenethyl-ITC. Returns ranked SMILES with QED + similarity + Lipinski + composite score.
examples: Generate SFN-class analogs from the current library
Generate phenethyl-isothiocyanate analogs with phenyl tether variants

skills.name: Multi-objective ranking across rescue / maintenance / remission
skills.description: Compute the composite hypothesis-ranking signal: 0.30 evidence + 0.35 weighted target similarity + 0.10 QED (generated only) + 0.10 warhead score + 0.15 safety bonus + small contextual BBB term. Returns ranked CSVs per category and auto-updates the Top-10 tables in hypotheses/*.md.
examples: Rank the current library + generated analogs for remission
Show me the top 10 maintenance candidates with their safety profile

skills.name: Create a standardized experiment report
skills.description: Scaffold a new experiments/EXP-NNN-*.md report from the standard 11-section template, prefilled with hypothesis, method, inputs, parameters, environment, outputs, interpretation, reproduction, limitations, and next-experiment suggestions. Updates the experiments index.
examples: Create an experiment report for a KEAP1 docking run on the top-50
Scaffold EXP-006 for a ChEMBL mast cell bioassay import

skills.name: Propose or critique a hypothesis
skills.description: Submit a falsifiable hypothesis (rescue / maintenance / remission / injury / trigger / cross-cutting) with falsifiable predictions, linked compounds and triggers, proposed in-silico tests, and proposed wet-lab tests. Opens a GitHub issue using the hypothesis_proposal template.
examples: Propose that PEITC + SCFA synergistically restore the gut-mast axis
Critique the SFN remission hypothesis with a falsifying experiment
